_description: IBM Quantum job IDs for all hardware experiments. All jobs run on IBM Fez (ibm_fez, 156-qubit Heron r2).

jobs.description: 16 error patterns x 2 logical states, 4 syndrome + 5 data qubits
jobs.purpose: Primary experiment: holographic code with perfect-tensor geometry. Tests whether DBCI's backward boundary reveals holographic structure (anisotropic DeltaH across stabilizer subsets, complementary recovery).
Surface code scaling baseline. Used in DeltaH, erasure tolerance, overhead reduction, and threshold analyses.
Surface code scaling baseline (logical |1> complement of d=3 L0).
Primary surface code distance for DBCI vs MWPM comparison (5.7x error reduction), erasure tolerance, and per-qubit importance analysis.

jobs.description: Both logical states in one job, 24 syndrome + 49 data qubits, 8000 shots each
jobs.purpose: Largest surface code distance. Tests scaling to 49 qubits and cross-distance validation of DBCI advantage and erasure tolerance.

jobs.description: 22 error patterns x 2 logical states, 6 syndrome + 7 data qubits
jobs.purpose: First non-holographic control (CSS/Hamming symmetry). Tests whether the isotropy gap is unique to holographic geometry. Result: CV ~0.2% (isotropic), confirming the gap.

jobs.description: 22 error patterns x 2 logical states, 8 syndrome + 9 data qubits
jobs.purpose: Second non-holographic control (concatenated, degenerate Z sector). Structurally independent from Steane. Result: CV ~3.0% (isotropic), strengthening attribution of anisotropy to holographic geometry.
